description: Systematic root cause analysis — severity triage, evidence gathering, root cause identification, targeted fix

severityAssessment.triageRationale: Memory usage grows linearly with request count, no GC reclamation observed

evidence.description: Heap snapshot at T+0h shows 120MB baseline, T+4h shows 1.8GB
evidence.finding: EventListener array in ConnectionPool grows unbounded

evidence.description: Correlated GC logs with request logs over 24h window
evidence.finding: GC pause times increase from 50ms to 2s, finalizer queue depth grows monotonically
release() removes connection from active set but does not detach error event listener registered in acquire()

hypotheses.hypothesis: Connection pool leaks event listeners on release
hypotheses.evidence: Heap snapshot shows EventListener[] referencing closed connections via error handler closure

hypotheses.hypothesis: Request body streams not being consumed
hypotheses.evidence: Added stream.destroy() calls but leak persists at same rate

investigation.rootCause: ConnectionPool.release() does not call connection.removeAllListeners('error') before returning connection to idle pool, causing closure references to accumulate

trace_events.description: Added removeAllListeners('error') in ConnectionPool.release() before returning to idle pool

description: Guess-and-fix — jumps straight to fix without investigation or severity assessment

trace_events.description: Changed password hashing from bcrypt to argon2 because someone said it might help

description: Partial investigation — some evidence gathered but root cause guessed without full analysis

severityAssessment.impact: API response times degraded 3x during peak hours
severityAssessment.triageRationale: Not a crash but significant UX degradation

evidence.description: Checked slow query log for queries over 500ms
evidence.finding: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id = ? takes 1.2s average

hypotheses.evidence: Seems likely based on the slow query but did not verify with EXPLAIN or check existing indexes

investigation.rootCause: Probably missing index on orders.user_id (not verified)

description: Thorough track with comprehensive evidence — multiple hypotheses tested, stack traces analyzed, bisect performed, root cause proven

severityAssessment.impact: 30% of payment webhooks silently dropped, revenue loss confirmed
severityAssessment.triageRationale: Data loss with financial impact, immediate investigation required

evidence.description: Captured stack trace from webhook handler error boundary
evidence.finding: TypeError: Cannot read property 'merchantId' of undefined at WebhookProcessor.validate (line 47)

evidence.description: Correlated webhook delivery logs with payment events over 7-day window
evidence.finding: Failures started exactly at deploy v2.14.0, 100% correlation with payload schema v3 webhooks

evidence.description: Git bisect across 23 commits between v2.13.0 and v2.14.0
evidence.finding: Commit abc123f introduced PayloadValidator refactor that changed nested object access from payload.merchant.id to payload.merchantId without updating webhook schema

evidence.description: Replayed 50 failed webhooks against v2.13.0 and v2.14.0
evidence.finding: All 50 succeed on v2.13.0, all 50 fail on v2.14.0 with same TypeError

evidence.description: Compared webhook payload schema v2 vs v3
evidence.finding: v3 schema flattened merchant object but PayloadValidator.validate() still expects nested merchant.id path

hypotheses.evidence: Rate limit headers show 0% throttling, all failures are 500 errors not 429
TLS handshake succeeds, failure occurs at application layer after full request parsing

hypotheses.hypothesis: PayloadValidator assumes nested merchant object but schema v3 flattened it
hypotheses.evidence: Bisect isolated commit abc123f, request replay confirms 100% correlation, schema diff proves structural mismatch

investigation.rootCause: PayloadValidator.validate() accesses payload.merchant.id (nested path) but webhook schema v3 flattened this to payload.merchantId — commit abc123f updated the schema but not the validator

bisectResult.description: PayloadValidator refactor changed schema without updating accessor path

trace_events.cause: PayloadValidator schema/accessor mismatch after v3 flattening

trace_events.description: Updated PayloadValidator.validate() to use payload.merchantId and added schema version detection with backward-compatible fallback
